content.assessment: This fiscal-lever approach is procedurally more contained (no direct admissions mandate) and leverages existing ABC testing/background-analysis infrastructure as a built-in monitoring mechanism, giving it a plausible measurement backbone. However, the same historical pattern of using assessment data for legitimization without structural follow-through raises a specific implementation risk: that fiscal conditionality becomes symbolic rather than binding, absent a defined enforcement or adaptation mechanism.
opportunities: Could pilot conditional funding in a limited set of maintainers before wider rollout, using existing grade 6/8/10 testing cycles as natural monitoring checkpoints.
strengths: Existing ABC testing infrastructure with mandatory family-background analysis provides a ready-made monitoring apparatus that could operationalize fiscal conditionality without building new measurement systems from scratch.
threats: No enforcement or escalation mechanism is described for cases where fiscal signals are ignored, risking a repeat of the symbolic-use pattern seen with PISA data.
weaknesses: Historical precedent shows assessment/PISA data has been used for legitimization without producing structural policy follow-through, directly threatening the credibility of any conditionality regime built on the same infrastructure.
